title: rubus lineatus
intro: Rubus lineatus, also known as the Japanese wineberry, is a species of bramble in the rose family, native to East Asia and known for its edible and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Rubus lineatus include its ability to support cardiovascular health, improve immune function, and act as an antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of Rubus lineatus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to make jams and jellies due to its sweet and tart flavor.
